'''Christopher Green''' (born 3 January 1990) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]] and spent time on loan at from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[2011 Championship 1|Championship 1]] and the [[2014 RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[York City Knights]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Green has also spent time on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] in the [[2019 Super League]].

==Background==
Green was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England.

==Career==
===Hull FC===
He has played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] (two [[Loan (sports)|loan]] spells), the [[York City Knights]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), and [[Featherstone Rovers]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), as a {{rlp|pr}}.

He is well known by fans after his 79th minute try against local rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at the [[Magic Weekend]] in 2013, in which [[Hull F.C.]] went on to win the game 22–16.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/derby-Hull-FC-beat-Rovers-22-16-dramatic-late/story-19088899-detail/story.html|title=The derby: Hull FC beat Rovers 22–16 after dramatic late comeback|date=25 May 2013|work=Hull Daily Mail|accessdate=4 September 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130614041027/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/derby-Hull-FC-beat-Rovers-22-16-dramatic-late/story-19088899-detail/story.html|archive-date=14 June 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|work=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 15 Oct 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Chris Green excited for Chris Chester reunion at Leigh|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/chris-green-excited-for-chris-chester-reunion-at-leigh/}}</ref>
